### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer â€” every 2 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `jobId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: job.start, job.done + fallback.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Handle 'job.start' â†’ Airtable Â· â‘¡â†’ Handle 'job.done' â†’ Forward API Â· â‘¢â†’ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'job.start' â†’ Airtable

Upserts into Airtable's 'Jobs' table, matched on `jobId` â€” safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Consolidate Handling Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

### Handle 'job.done' â†’ Forward API

Ports: â‘ â†’ Consolidate Handling Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Jobs' table.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Consolidate Handling Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Airtable PAT

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Searches Airtable's 'Jobs' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Compute Drift vs Snapshot Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Airtable PAT

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Jobs' table.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Fingerprint for Idempotency Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Airtable PAT

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per job (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats â€” the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

Ports: â‘ â†’ Fold In Enrichment Fields Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record â€” searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Jobs' table.

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook â€” no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#automation`) â€” a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' â€” a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

# Streamline a routine task with HTTP / REST API and Airtable in real time

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation Â· **Level:** Production Â· **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Streamline a routine task with HTTP / REST API and Airtable in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual process orchestration is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule â†’ Map to Canonical Schema â†’ Route by Event Kind â†’ Handle 'job.start' â†’ Airtable â†’ Consolidate Handling â†’ Load Previous Snapshot â†’ Compute Drift vs Snapshot â†’ â€¦

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating process orchestration in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Airtable PAT; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: ðŸŸ¦ entry Â· ðŸŸª AI Â· ðŸŸ« actions Â· â¬œ utilities Â· ðŸŸ© outputs Â· ðŸŸ¥ error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath â€” read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger â€” hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click â†’ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload â€” bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade â€” deepen your understanding:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## ðŸ›Ÿ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3Ã— with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail â†’ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ðŸ§¹ Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node â†’ 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## ðŸ›  Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ðŸ”‘ lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your process orchestration process. Every node card explains its role â€” safe to extend at any marked branch.
